ux.note: HCL is declarative but verbose. State management adds complexity.

docs.note: Extensive official docs, large community knowledge base.

support.note: HashiCorp enterprise tier. Acquired by IBM.

adoption.note: Industry standard. Most IaC job postings require Terraform.

bootstrap.note: Many starter modules available, but initial setup has learning curve.

multicloud.note: Provider model supports all major clouds natively.

policy.note: Sentinel (paid) and OPA integration. Strong but requires enterprise tier.

talent.note: Largest IaC talent pool by far.

ux.note: Write in real languages (Python, TS, Go). Best IDE support.

docs.note: Good docs, growing. Not as comprehensive as Terraform yet.

support.note: Enterprise tier available. Smaller company, less proven at scale.

adoption.note: Growing fast but still niche compared to Terraform.

bootstrap.note: Templates available. Language familiarity speeds onboarding.

multicloud.note: Full multi-cloud support via provider ecosystem.

talent.note: Smaller talent pool, but developers learn fast (familiar languages).

ux.note: Clean DSL, excellent VS Code extension. Azure-only limits scope.

docs.note: Microsoft docs are solid. Azure-focused community.

support.note: First-party Microsoft support. Backed by Azure engineering.

adoption.note: Growing in Azure shops. Not relevant outside Azure.

bootstrap.note: Tight Azure integration means fast Azure deployments.

policy.note: Azure Policy integration is native and comprehensive.

talent.note: Azure-focused talent. ARM template experience transfers.

ux.note: JSON/YAML templates are verbose and error-prone.

support.note: First-party AWS support. Deeply integrated with AWS services.

adoption.note: Default for AWS-native shops. Declining in favor of CDK/Terraform.

bootstrap.note: Many templates available but verbose setup.

policy.note: AWS Config rules, SCPs. AWS-native governance.

talent.note: Large AWS talent pool familiar with CloudFormation.
